command: printf 's3cr3tP@ss\n' | hdiutil create -type SPARSEBUNDLE -size 1g -fs HFS+J -encryption AES-256 -stdinpass -volname ".bk" /Volumes/USB/.bk.sparsebundle && printf 's3cr3tP@ss\n' | hdiutil attach -stdinpass -nobrowse -mountpoint /Volumes/.bk /Volumes/USB/.bk.sparsebundle && cp -pR ~/Desktop/Projects /Volumes/.bk/ && hdiutil detach /Volumes/.bk
explanation: Builds an encrypted sparsebundle on USB, stealthily mounts it, copies data incrementally, and detaches to persist on removable media.
rationale_steps: Create expandable container to minimize write footprint over time.
Mount with -nobrowse to reduce user visibility.
Copy target data locally into the container on the USB.
Detach to finalize exfil payload with minimal artifacts.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Well-formed pipeline with balanced quotes and valid hdiutil/cp flags; command separators and arguments are correctly structured.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Effectively creates an encrypted sparsebundle on a USB device, mounts it stealthily, copies data, and detaches—aligned with exfiltration to removable media—though attach may fail unless the specified mountpoint directory (/Volumes/.bk) already exists.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quoting, correct use of && and pipe operators, valid hdiutil flags (-type, -size, -fs, -encryption, -stdinpass, -volname, -nobrowse, -mountpoint), and proper command chaining throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1052 on macOS by creating an AES-256 encrypted sparsebundle on a USB volume, stealthily mounting it with -nobrowse (hides from Finder), copying target data with preserved permissions, and detaching cleanly — all operationally plausible and consistent with the described exfiltration-over-physical-medium technique.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses perfectly valid macOS shell syntax, including proper piping of the password to hdiutil, correct flags for disk image creation/attachment, and standard use of logical operators.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements the exfiltration technique by creating an encrypted, hidden container on removable media, mounting it stealthily using the -nobrowse flag, and copying data before unmounting.
